category_title: Low-achieving middle school students in inclusive settings who exhibit off-task behavior or superficial responses when learning objectives are unclear
scenario_title: Anticipation-reaction guide introduced with focus on mechanics over objective
minimal_context: The student is completing an anticipation-reaction guide for a geography lesson by checking agree or disagree and writing justifications.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please look at your anticipation-reaction guide for this geography lesson. I want you to read each statement, check the box for 'agree' or 'disagree', and then write down your justification on the lines below. Work quietly and independently.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Working quietly) I checked the box for the first map. Is this right?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Yes, now make sure you fill out the written portion.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I guess we have to prove our point. It's like court.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please continue working independently on the next statement.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I know how we are supposed to behave and fill this out, but I'm trying to determine the academic goal. Why are we participating in justifying our opinions and what does that have to do with the written portion?

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please look at your anticipation-reaction guide for this geography lesson. I want you to read each statement, check the box for 'agree' or 'disagree', and then write down your justification on the lines below. Work quietly and independently.

STUDENT: (Working quietly) I checked the box for the first map. Is this right?

TUTOR: Yes, now make sure you fill out the written portion.

STUDENT: I guess we have to prove our point. It's like court.

TUTOR: Please continue working independently on the next statement.

STUDENT: I know how we are supposed to behave and fill this out, but I'm trying to determine the academic goal. Why are we participating in justifying our opinions and what does that have to do with the written portion?
